title: ptelea trifoliata var. mollis
intro: Ptelea trifoliata var. mollis, also known as the Fragrant Horsemint or White Horsemint, is a variety of the Ptelea trifoliata plant, a deciduous shrub native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ptelea trifoliata var. mollis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a topical application for skin conditions.
